name: Whitney Houston
description: American singer, actress, and film producer (1963–2012)
abstract: Whitney Elizabeth Houston (August 9, 1963 – February 11, 2012) was an American singer, actress, film producer, and record producer. Commonly referred to as "the Voice", she is one of the most awarded performers of all time.

concerts.label: Rainforest Foundation Benefit Concert: An Eclectic Night Out
Benefit: Wyclef Jean Foundation
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Byron Stripling
about_quotes.evidence: Do you need to be just more attentive as you listen to the radio so you can sing that new song “And I Will Always Love You” Whitney Houston you know?
about_quotes.doc_title: Byron Stripling interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 25, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bela Fleck
about_quotes.evidence: a fellow named Kashif who ... produced I guess Whitney Houston and some other people
about_quotes.doc_title: Bela Fleck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 7, 2004

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Blue
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...reminded me a little bit of Whitney Houston...
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Blue interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 23, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: I played on one... I played fills behind her... You played on my record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ingrid Sertso
about_quotes.evidence: When Whitney Houston died, Whitney Houston? I totally adored her. She was, for me, the purist Gospel singer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ingrid Sertso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Hey
about_quotes.evidence: even if you’re Celine Dion or Whitney Houston or anything, it’s David is in charge there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Hey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 2, 2023
